## 7. Content Strategy

### 7.1 Blog / SEO Content Calendar

ADL needs to own search terms that B2B buyers use when looking for African location data. Target keywords and content plan:

| Priority | Target Keyword | Search Intent | Blog Post Title | Language |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "pharmacy locations Douala" | Local/commercial | "Every Pharmacy in Bonamoussadi, Douala: A Complete Map (2026)" | EN + FR |
| 2 | "hyperlocal data Africa" | Informational/commercial | "Why Hyperlocal Data Is the Missing Infrastructure Layer in Africa" | EN |
| 3 | "mobile money agent map Cameroon" | Commercial | "Mapping Every Mobile Money Agent in Douala: What We Found" | EN + FR |
| 4 | "Africa POI data API" | Commercial/transactional | "How to Access Verified Point-of-Interest Data for African Cities via API" | EN |
| 5 | "crowdsourcing data Africa" | Informational | "Crowdsourcing vs. Traditional Surveys: How Africa's Data Gap Is Being Closed" | EN |
| 6 | "donnees geographiques Afrique" | Informational | "Cartographier l'Afrique par le bas : comment les contributeurs locaux construisent la couche de donnees qui manquait" | FR |
| 7 | "fuel station locations Cameroon" | Local/commercial | "Fuel Station Coverage in Douala: What Google Maps Misses" | EN + FR |
| 8 | "retail audit Africa" | Commercial | "The $0.10 Retail Audit: How Crowdsourced Data Disrupts Traditional Market Research in Africa" | EN |
| 9 | "informal economy data Africa" | Informational | "The Invisible Economy: Why Informal Businesses Are Missing from Every Map of Africa" | EN |
| 10 | "OpenStreetMap Africa coverage" | Informational | "OpenStreetMap in Africa: What It Gets Right, What It Misses, and How to Fill the Gaps" | EN |

**Publishing cadence:** 2 posts/month during Phase 1, scaling to weekly in Phase 2.

**Content production process:** Founder writes first draft from ADL data insights. Each post includes real data (e.g., "We found 87 pharmacies in a neighborhood where Google Maps shows 4"). Real data is the content moat -- competitors cannot produce this content without doing the fieldwork.

---

### 7.2 Social Proof Assets

These assets convert skeptics at every stage of the funnel.

| Asset | Purpose | Production Plan |
|---|---|---|
| **Contributor earnings screenshots** | Recruit new contributors. "Look, real people earning real money." | Ask top 5 contributors for permission to share anonymized earnings. Post weekly in WhatsApp and Instagram. |
| **Before/after map comparison** | B2B sales. "Google Maps shows 4 pharmacies. ADL shows 87." | Generate side-by-side screenshots from Google Maps vs ADL for Bonamoussadi. Use in LinkedIn posts, pitch decks, blog posts. |
| **Data quality metrics dashboard** | Investor/B2B credibility. "Our data is verified and fresh." | Publish monthly transparency report: verification rate, freshness score, contributor count, fraud rejection rate. |
| **Contributor video testimonials** | Emotional resonance. "Hear from Amina, a student who earns 20,000 FCFA/month with ADL." | Record 3-minute phone videos with top contributors. Use in Facebook/Instagram ads and website. |
| **B2B case study** | Enterprise sales. "How [Company X] reduced delivery failures by 40% using ADL data." | Write after first B2B pilot completes. Even a 1-page case study with real metrics is high-value. |
| **Neighborhood coverage reports** | Community pride + B2B pipeline. "Bonamoussadi is now the best-mapped neighborhood in Cameroon." | Generate automatically from ADL data. Share with community leaders and local press. |

### 7.3 Developer Documentation

API documentation is a sales channel for technical buyers. Key sections:

| Section | Content | Priority |

| **Changelog** | Coverage updates, new categories, API changes. | Ongoing |

**Hosting:** Start with a dedicated `/docs` page on the ADL site or a free-tier ReadMe.io/GitBook site. Migrate to custom docs site when API revenue justifies it.

## Appendix B: Key Cameroonian Market Context

| Factor | Detail | Implication for ADL |
|---|---|---|
| **Internet penetration** | ~45% (2025), heavily mobile. MTN and Orange dominate. | Mobile-first PWA is the right form factor. |
| **Smartphone penetration** | ~55% in urban areas, predominantly low-end Android (Tecno, Itel). | App must work on 1-2 GB RAM devices. Offline-first is essential. |
| **Mobile money** | MTN MoMo and Orange Money are the primary payment rails. 70%+ of urban adults use mobile money. | Payout via MoMo/OM is the only viable contributor payment method. |
| **Language** | French dominant in Douala, English in Northwest/Southwest, Pidgin as lingua franca. | All contributor-facing content must be bilingual (FR/EN). Pidgin for informal communications (WhatsApp, TikTok). |
| **Youth unemployment** | ~60% underemployment among 18-35 in urban areas. | Strong pull for any legitimate earning opportunity. Messaging: income stability > gig economy hype. |
| **Social media usage** | Facebook and WhatsApp dominate. Instagram growing among youth. Twitter/X used by tech/business community. TikTok growing fast among 15-25. | WhatsApp is the primary community channel. Facebook for paid acquisition. TikTok for organic youth reach. |
| **Trust dynamics** | Word-of-mouth and personal referrals are the strongest trust signals. "My friend uses it" > "I saw an ad." | Campus ambassador and community leader strategies are higher-trust than digital ads. |
| **Payment expectations** | Small, frequent payments preferred over large, delayed ones. Consistency matters more than amount (confirmed by Kasi meeting). | Weekly payouts, not monthly. Even 2,000 FCFA/week is valuable if consistent. |
| **Competitor landscape** | No direct competitor doing hyperlocal crowdsourced POI data in Cameroon. OSM presence is minimal. Google Maps coverage is basic. | First-mover advantage in Cameroon. Speed of execution matters more than features. |

> 1. Inscris-toi en 30 secondes (lien ci-dessous)
> 2. Photographie et localise un commerce pres de toi
> 3. Gagne des XP et recois ton paiement chaque vendredi sur MoMo/OM
>
> Ca marche meme sans connexion internet!
>
> Rejoins les +50 contributeurs de Bonamoussadi: [lien]
>
> Des questions? Ecris ici, on repond en moins de 2h.

### Flyer Text (Front)

> **GAGNEZ DE L'ARGENT AVEC VOTRE TELEPHONE**
>
> Cartographiez les commerces de votre quartier et gagnez chaque semaine.
>
> - 5 minutes par contribution
> - Paiement chaque vendredi sur MoMo/OM
> - Fonctionne meme hors connexion
>
> Scannez le QR code pour commencer >>
>
> **EARN MONEY WITH YOUR PHONE**
>
> Map the businesses in your neighborhood and earn every week.

### Radio Script (30 seconds, French)

> Vous connaissez votre quartier mieux que n'importe quelle carte. Les pharmacies, les kiosques MoMo, les stations-service -- vous savez ou ils sont. Avec African Data Layer, cette connaissance vous rapporte de l'argent reel. Inscrivez-vous gratuitement, cartographiez les commerces autour de vous, et recevez votre paiement chaque vendredi sur MTN MoMo ou Orange Money. Ca marche meme sans internet. African Data Layer -- vos connaissances de quartier ont de la valeur. Rendez-vous sur africandatalayer.com.

---

*This document is immediately actionable. Every experiment has a budget, timeline, and decision criteria. Start with Week 1, Day 1: finalize messaging and create the WhatsApp community. Everything else follows.*

data.prompt:
You are **Teammate 7 — Marketing Genius** for the African Data Layer (ADL) platform.

## Platform Context
ADL is a mobile-first crowdsourcing platform for hyperlocal data in Africa. Field agents and community members contribute real-time data about pharmacies, mobile money kiosks, and fuel stations. Contributors earn XP and rewards. The platform started in Bonamoussadi, Douala, Cameroon and is expanding across Africa.

**Key value props:**
- Crowdsourced hyperlocal data enrichment for Africa
- Offline-first design for low-connectivity areas
- Gamified incentives for contributors
- Fraud detection ensures data quality
- Bilingual (English/French)
- Categories: Pharmacies, Mobile Money, Fuel Stations

## Predecessor Outputs — Read at minimum docs 01 and 06 for context
1. `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/team/01-cloud-architecture.md`
2. `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/team/06-data-analytics.md`

Also read if it exists:
- `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/pitch-one-pager-kasi-insight.md` — existing pitch doc

## Your Deliverable
Write to `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/team/07-marketing-strategy.md`.

Cover:

### 1. Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs)
Define 3-4 distinct ICPs:

**ICP 1 — Data Consumers (B2B buyers):** Who buys this data? Logistics companies, fintechs, telcos, NGOs, government agencies, ride-hailing apps, delivery services operating in Africa. For each: pain point, buying trigger, budget, decision maker, champion.

**ICP 2 — Field Contributors (Supply side):** University students, gig workers, community leaders, local entrepreneurs. For each: motivation, typical device, connectivity, daily routine.

**ICP 3 — Investor/Partner Audience:** Impact investors, development finance institutions, tech accelerators focused on Africa.

### 2. Personas
Create 5 detailed personas (give them African names):
- A university student in Douala contributing for extra income
- A mobile money agent enriching competitor data
- A logistics startup PM needing point-of-service data
- A fintech product manager expanding to Cameroon
- A development agency program officer

For each: name, age, location, device, goals, frustrations, how they discover ADL, what makes them stay/leave.

### 3. Positioning & Messaging
- **Positioning statement** (for contributors, for data buyers, for investors)
- **Tagline options** (5 options, English and French)
- **Value proposition canvas** (jobs, pains, gains for each segment)
- **Competitive differentiation** vs. OpenStreetMap, Google Maps, proprietary data providers, other crowdsourcing platforms
- **Messaging framework** by channel and audience

### 4. Channel Strategy & Experiments
For each channel, define: hypothesis, experiment design, budget, timeline, success metric, decision criteria.

**Digital channels:**
- WhatsApp groups/communities (key in Cameroon)
- Facebook/Instagram ads targeting Douala/Yaoundé
- Twitter/X for B2B data consumers
- LinkedIn for investors and enterprise buyers
- University campus digital boards
- TikTok for young contributor recruitment

**Offline channels:**
- University campus ambassadors
- Mobile money agent networks
- Church/community group presentations
- Radio spots (local stations in Douala)
- Flyer distribution at fuel stations and pharmacies

**B2B channels:**
- LinkedIn outreach to logistics/fintech PMs
- Partnership with mobile money operators (MTN MoMo, Orange Money)
- Conference presence (AfricaCom, Africa Tech Summit)
- API documentation and developer relations

### 5. Growth Metrics & Funnel
Define the full funnel with conversion targets:
- **Awareness:** Impressions, reach
- **Acquisition:** App installs / signups (target: X/week)
- **Activation:** First contribution within 24h
- **Retention:** D7/D30 active contributor rate
- **Revenue:** Data API subscriptions, enterprise deals
- **Referral:** Viral coefficient, invite mechanism

### 6. Growth Playbook (First 90 Days)
Week-by-week plan:
- Weeks 1-2: Foundation (messaging, assets, WhatsApp group setup)
- Weeks 3-4: Bonamoussadi campus blitz (50 contributors)
- Weeks 5-6: Expand to 2 more Douala neighborhoods
- Weeks 7-8: First B2B outreach (10 warm leads)
- Weeks 9-12: Scale what works, kill what doesn't

### 7. Content Strategy
- Blog topics (SEO for "Africa data", "hyperlocal data Africa", etc.)
- Social proof assets (contributor stories, data quality metrics)
- Developer documentation for API consumers
- Investor materials (pitch deck outline, data room structure)

Be specific to the African/Cameroonian context. Include French translations where relevant. This should be immediately actionable.

content.text:
The marketing strategy document has been written to `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/team/07-marketing-strategy.md`.

Here is a summary of what the document covers:

### 1. Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs)
- **ICP 1 -- Data Consumers (B2B):** Four sub-segments with full detail: FMCG/beverage companies needing retail audits (highest value, confirmed by Kasi Insight meeting), fintech/mobile money operators, logistics/delivery companies, and NGOs/development agencies/government. Each includes pain point, buying trigger, budget range in USD and FCFA, decision maker, and champion.
- **ICP 2 -- Field Contributors:** Four sub-segments: university students, gig workers/moto-taxi drivers, mobile money agents, and community leaders. Each includes motivation, typical device, connectivity reality, and acquisition channel.
- **ICP 3 -- Investors & Partners:** Impact investors/DFIs and tech accelerators with what they need to see before committing.

### 2. Five Detailed Personas
- **Amina Tchinda** (21, university student, Bonamoussadi) -- earns side income mapping pharmacies between classes
- **Emmanuel Nkeng** (34, MTN MoMo agent, Carrefour Ange Raphael) -- maps competitor agents during slow hours
- **Fatou Diallo** (29, logistics PM, Dakar) -- needs POI API to reduce delivery failures
- **Thierry Mbarga** (32, fintech PM, Lagos) -- expanding to Cameroon, needs agent density data
- **Carine Mengue** (38, development agency program officer, Yaounde) -- mapping pharmacies for health program

Each persona includes device, goals, frustrations, discovery path, first experience, retention triggers, and churn risks.

### 3. Positioning & Messaging
- Three positioning statements (contributors, B2B buyers, investors) in English and French
- Five tagline options with French translations (recommended: "Your neighborhood knowledge has value" for contributors, "The data layer Africa was missing" for B2B/investors)
- Value proposition canvas for all three segments
- Competitive differentiation matrix vs. OpenStreetMap, Google Maps, Nielsen/Kantar, and Premise/mSurvey
- Messaging framework by channel and audience (WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, radio, campus, email)

### 4. Channel Strategy & Experiments
Sixteen experiments across three categories, each with hypothesis, design, budget, timeline, success metric, and kill/scale criteria:
- **Digital (6):** WhatsApp community, Facebook/Instagram ads, Twitter/X threads, LinkedIn outreach, university digital boards, TikTok content
- **Offline (5):** Campus ambassadors, MoMo agent network seeding, church/community presentations, local radio spots, flyer distribution
- **B2B (4):** LinkedIn sales outreach, mobile money operator partnerships, conference presence, developer relations/API docs

### 5. Growth Metrics & Funnel
- Full contributor funnel (awareness through referral) with Phase 1 and Phase 2 targets
- Full B2B buyer funnel (awareness through advocacy) with targets
- North Star Metric: Active Verified Data Points per Region (aligned with KPI-T1-01 from the analytics framework)
- Six key ratios to monitor weekly with healthy ranges and red flags

### 6. Growth Playbook (First 90 Days)
Week-by-week execution plan across four phases:
- **Weeks 1-2:** Foundation (messaging, WhatsApp setup, flyer design, tracking infrastructure)
- **Weeks 3-4:** Bonamoussadi campus blitz (target: 50 contributors)
- **Weeks 5-6:** Expand to Akwa and Deido (target: 100 contributors, 200 verified points)
- **Weeks 7-8:** First B2B outreach (target: 10 warm leads)
- **Weeks 9-12:** Scale what works, kill what doesn't, close first B2B pilot

Week 12 success criteria table with explicit red flags.

### 7. Content Strategy
- SEO content calendar with 10 target keywords and blog post titles (English and French)
- Six social proof assets (earnings screenshots, before/after maps, data quality dashboards, video testimonials, case studies, coverage reports)
- Developer documentation plan with seven sections and weekly shipping timeline
- Pitch deck outline (12 slides) and data room structure for investor readiness

### Appendices
- **Budget summary:** Total 90-day marketing budget of 1,885,000 FCFA (~$3,016 USD), with contributor payouts as the largest line item
- **Cameroonian market context:** Internet/smartphone penetration, mobile money dominance, language dynamics, youth unemployment, trust dynamics, payment expectations
- **French translations:** Ready-to-use WhatsApp signup message, bilingual flyer text, and 30-second radio script in French

- Social proof assets (contributor stories, data quality metrics)
- Developer documentation for API consumers
- Investor materials (pitch deck outline, data room structure)

Be specific to the African/Cameroonian context. Include French translations where relevant. This should be immediately actionable.

content.text:
The marketing strategy document has been written to `/Users/charlesvictormahouve/Documents/GitHub/africandatalayer/docs/team/07-marketing-strategy.md`.

Here is a summary of what the document covers:

### 1. Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs)
- **ICP 1 -- Data Consumers (B2B):** Four sub-segments with full detail: FMCG/beverage companies needing retail audits (highest value, confirmed by Kasi Insight meeting), fintech/mobile money operators, logistics/delivery companies, and NGOs/development agencies/government. Each includes pain point, buying trigger, budget range in USD and FCFA, decision maker, and champion.
- **ICP 2 -- Field Contributors:** Four sub-segments: university students, gig workers/moto-taxi drivers, mobile money agents, and community leaders. Each includes motivation, typical device, connectivity reality, and acquisition channel.
- **ICP 3 -- Investors & Partners:** Impact investors/DFIs and tech accelerators with what they need to see before committing.

### 2. Five Detailed Personas
- **Amina Tchinda** (21, university student, Bonamoussadi) -- earns side income mapping pharmacies between classes
- **Emmanuel Nkeng** (34, MTN MoMo agent, Carrefour Ange Raphael) -- maps competitor agents during slow hours
- **Fatou Diallo** (29, logistics PM, Dakar) -- needs POI API to reduce delivery failures
- **Thierry Mbarga** (32, fintech PM, Lagos) -- expanding to Cameroon, needs agent density data
- **Carine Mengue** (38, development agency program officer, Yaounde) -- mapping pharmacies for health program

Each persona includes device, goals, frustrations, discovery path, first experience, retention triggers, and churn risks.

### 3. Positioning & Messaging
- Three positioning statements (contributors, B2B buyers, investors) in English and French
- Five tagline options with French translations (recommended: "Your neighborhood knowledge has value" for contributors, "The data layer Africa was missing" for B2B/investors)
- Value proposition canvas for all three segments
- Competitive differentiation matrix vs. OpenStreetMap, Google Maps, Nielsen/Kantar, and Premise/mSurvey
- Messaging framework by channel and audience (WhatsApp, Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter, radio, campus, email)

### 4. Channel Strategy & Experiments
Sixteen experiments across three categories, each with hypothesis, design, budget, timeline, success metric, and kill/scale criteria:
- **Digital (6):** WhatsApp community, Facebook/Instagram ads, Twitter/X threads, LinkedIn outreach, university digital boards, TikTok content
- **Offline (5):** Campus ambassadors, MoMo agent network seeding, church/community presentations, local radio spots, flyer distribution
- **B2B (4):** LinkedIn sales outreach, mobile money operator partnerships, conference presence, developer relations/API docs

### 5. Growth Metrics & Funnel
- Full contributor funnel (awareness through referral) with Phase 1 and Phase 2 targets
- Full B2B buyer funnel (awareness through advocacy) with targets
- North Star Metric: Active Verified Data Points per Region (aligned with KPI-T1-01 from the analytics framework)
- Six key ratios to monitor weekly with healthy ranges and red flags

### 6. Growth Playbook (First 90 Days)
Week-by-week execution plan across four phases:
- **Weeks 1-2:** Foundation (messaging, WhatsApp setup, flyer design, tracking infrastructure)
- **Weeks 3-4:** Bonamoussadi campus blitz (target: 50 contributors)
- **Weeks 5-6:** Expand to Akwa and Deido (target: 100 contributors, 200 verified points)
- **Weeks 7-8:** First B2B outreach (target: 10 warm leads)
- **Weeks 9-12:** Scale what works, kill what doesn't, close first B2B pilot

Week 12 success criteria table with explicit red flags.

### 7. Content Strategy
- SEO content calendar with 10 target keywords and blog post titles (English and French)
- Six social proof assets (earnings screenshots, before/after maps, data quality dashboards, video testimonials, case studies, coverage reports)
- Developer documentation plan with seven sections and weekly shipping timeline
- Pitch deck outline (12 slides) and data room structure for investor readiness

### Appendices
- **Budget summary:** Total 90-day marketing budget of 1,885,000 FCFA (~$3,016 USD), with contributor payouts as the largest line item
- **Cameroonian market context:** Internet/smartphone penetration, mobile money dominance, language dynamics, youth unemployment, trust dynamics, payment expectations
- **French translations:** Ready-to-use WhatsApp signup message, bilingual flyer text, and 30-second radio script in French

Every architectural decision is filtered through three lenses: **does it work offline?**, **does it work on a $50 phone with 1 GB RAM?**, and **does it survive 300 ms+ round-trip latency to the nearest PoP?**
    28→

    30→

    34→| P2 | **Offline-first is non-negotiable** | Contributors in Bonamoussadi and beyond face intermittent connectivity. The existing `lib/client/offlineQueue.ts` IndexedDB queue with exponential backoff already implements this; architecture must never regress it. |
